ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Shirley Tang.

The Court inquired about the steps that have been taken to enrich Ms. Chiu's life. Ms. Ng responded stating that Ms. Chiu is level two at the facility, that she is compliant with her medications, that she is not combative, that she attends groups, that she goes on outings, and receives a $40.00 weekly allowance. In addition, Ms. Ng reported that their was a surplus of funds/build up of benefits for Ms. Chiu and that there was no need for additional funds since she manages her money well. At the conclusion of Ms. Ng's report, the matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-30-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Shirley Tang (Trustee).

The Court stated that the bond would be reduced to $44,000.00 and noted that the bank letter has a different amount then the accounting. Ms. Ng stated that the difference in the letter and the accounting was due to interest; it can be adjusted at the time of the next accounting. The Court noted that the Conservator has not spent much money on the Conservatee. Ms. Ng stated that the Conservator of the Person is the City and County, that there is an LPS conservatorship, that the Special Needs Trust was established through the conservatorship, that they can't control where the Conservatee goes; it's a constant problem, that the Conservatee is now residing in Fresno, that the family can't get to her as much, and now it is a five hour drive for the family to visit with her. In addition, Ms. Ng stated that she had tried to get the Trust out from under Court supervision because it is being eaten up by fees but that didn't work. Ms. Ng addressed the issue of the family not spending much money on the Conservatee stating that the Conservatee leads a very simple life and that she does not ask for much. The Court granted the petition (an order for the same was signed in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
